content:
Users preferred the web version — the specific capabilities that closed every gap users used to complain about.

10 min read
1 hour ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

For three years I heard the same feedback.
“Why can’t the web app work offline?” “The native app is faster.” “I wish the web version sent notifications like the mobile app does.” “It doesn’t feel like a real app.” The complaints were consistent and they all pointed at the same gap — web apps felt like websites and native apps felt like software.
I started systematically closing that gap eighteen months ago. Not by rebuilding the application as a native app — by using JavaScript APIs that most web developers know exist and few use in production.
The results were measurable. User satisfaction scores for the web application, which had consistently trailed the native iOS app, crossed it within four months. Six months after that they surpassed it. The most common feedback shifted from “use the native app instead” to “I just use the web version now.”
Here’s exactly what changed and why each feature moved the needle.
1. Service Workers and Offline Functionality — The…
